steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The four $x$-intercepts of $y = f(x)$ are $x = -6,\ 0,\ 4,\ 7$. A polynomial $q(x)$ is a factor of $f(x)$ only if every root of $q(x)$ is also a root of $f(x)$. Check each choice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x^2 - 2x - 24$': factors as $(x-6)(x+4)$, roots $6$ and $-4$. Neither is in $\{-6, 0, 4, 7\}$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$x^2 - x + 42$': discriminant $= 1 - 168 < 0$, no real roots, so it cannot be a factor of a polynomial with four real zeros. ✗
  • Choice C — '$x^2 - 11x - 28$': roots $\approx 13.1$ and $\approx -2.1$, neither in the zero set. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x^2 - 7x$': factors as $x(x-7)$, roots $0$ and $7$. Both are zeros of $f$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
